title: Greater Exodus

summary.what: A reusable biblical theme that compares the first Exodus from Egypt with prophetic expectations of a later, greater regathering, deliverance, purification, and covenant restoration.
summary.whyItMatters: The theme can connect Exodus, prophetic restoration texts, Revelation, Sacred Time, commandments, maps, and timeline events without rewriting the same material in each system.
boundaries: This module records relationships and confidence labels; it does not decide a full eschatology.
Direct Scripture links are separated from typological, thematic, disputed, and speculative links.
Modern fulfillment claims are outside the core module unless added later as disputed source relationships.

primaryQuestions: How does the first Exodus become a pattern for later deliverance?
Which prophets speak of a future regathering greater than the Exodus from Egypt?
Where does Revelation echo Exodus language, plague patterns, wilderness protection, tribes, and the Song of Moses?
Which parts are direct biblical connections and which are interpretive or disputed?

confidenceScale.explicit: Directly stated by the cited text.
confidenceScale.strong: Strong relationship based on direct wording, repeated biblical pattern, or close canonical link.
confidenceScale.medium: Reasonable thematic or typological relationship, but not directly stated.
confidenceScale.disputed: Recognized interpretive disagreement or multiple competing readings.
confidenceScale.speculative: Possible association requiring review before reuse in study surfaces.

scriptures.label: Israel and mixed multitude leave Egypt
scriptures.note: Baseline first-Exodus pattern: Israel plus mixed multitude.

scriptures.label: The LORD fights while Israel holds peace
scriptures.note: Useful for silence/deliverance echoes, especially when paired cautiously with Revelation 8:1.

scriptures.label: Song of Moses after deliverance
scriptures.note: Canonical source for later Song of Moses connections.

scriptures.label: A future return greater than the Exodus from Egypt
A future return that reframes the Exodus memory
Gathering, wilderness judgment, and covenant discipline
scriptures.note: Strong thematic relationship to wilderness purification.

scriptures.label: Wilderness wooing and renewed covenant language
Second recovery and highway imagery
scriptures.note: Exodus-like return imagery across nations and geography.

sacredTime.title: Passover
sacredTime.note: The first Exodus is explicitly anchored to Passover.

sacredTime.title: Unleavened Bread
sacredTime.note: Departure and unleavened bread are directly linked in Exodus.

sacredTime.title: Yom Teruah / Trumpets
sacredTime.note: Useful for Revelation trumpet studies, but the Greater Exodus link is thematic.

sacredTime.title: Yom Kippur / Day of Atonement
sacredTime.note: Related through purification/judgment readings; exact fulfillment claims require review.

sacredTime.title: Sukkot / Tabernacles
sacredTime.note: Wilderness dwelling and ingathering make this a reusable but not forced relationship.

timelineEvents.title: Future Regathering
timelineEvents.note: Timeline placement depends on interpretive framework.

revelationChapters.label: Sealing of tribes and great multitude
Firstfruits, harvest, commandments, Babylon warning
revelationChapters.note: Important future integration target; app data should confirm chapter 14 exists before linking.

revelationChapters.label: Song of Moses and Song of the Lamb

commandments.title: Commandments in Revelation
commandments.note: Anchors include Revelation 12:17, 14:12, and 22:14; interpretation of scope requires care.

sources.title: The Book of Revelation Hebrew Roots
sources.note: Useful for identifying module relationships; not itself treated as final commentary.

maps.title: Scattering and Regathering
maps.note: Useful map layer, but specific routes and fulfillment geography need review.

reviewQueue.label: Modern fulfillment claims
reviewQueue.reason: The prototype intentionally excludes modern identification claims until sources and confidence rules are defined.

reviewQueue.label: Revelation 14 dependency
reviewQueue.reason: Revelation 14 is central to harvest/firstfruits/commandments links and should exist in NT data before integration.

reviewQueue.label: Fall feast sequence claims
reviewQueue.reason: Sacred Time links should stay relationship-based unless the project later adds a disputed fulfillment timeline.
